parsed_review.summary: CP00-494 is a descriptor-only governance-core slice (RP16.P05.M03.S07-RP16.P05.M05.S02, 40 units, risk B) and is supported by implementation, tests, and recorded local/Hermes validation. The pack is the live current pack in docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (actual_first_planned_pack_id=CP00-494, unit_count 40), and the manifest, review-baseline, contract p05_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor, GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP494 registry constants, validator script, and model.test.js all agree on counts (M03=16, M04=22, M05=2) and the deliverable distribution {claude_review:2, implementation:16, security_audit:4, fixture:10, test:6, hermes_evidence:2}. The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly false (status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready false, implementation_layer descriptor); no production_ready gate is weakened. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are verified by source inspection: service.js/validators.js/registry.js are pure Object.freeze descriptor builders, and a write/network/exec/db grep across packages/governance/src returned only '*: false' attestation flags. The validator deep-equals contract<->registry for current_pack, cp494_requirements, cp494_no_write_attestation and the descriptor, asserts descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false per row, asserts authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, and confirms CP00-494 is the last historical_pack_binding. Claude remains read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only true) and not final approver; the human enterprise-trust gate is preserved. Hermes is used only as a deterministic review-packet/evidence builder (no runtime receipt; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false). Auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte stdout); no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped evidence detected. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are legitimately pending this review per the documented hardening sequence and do not block. One non-blocking P3 naming observation is recorded; no P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: authority_boundary.runtime_opening_pack_id names the next pack while CP00-494 stays descriptor-only
findings.details: In createGovernanceCoreCp494P05ImplementationWorkflowSliceDescriptor the authority_boundary sets runtime_opening_pack_id to GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP494_PACK_BINDING.next_pack_id (CP00-495), mirrored in contracts/governance-core-contract.json:16821. The prior pack CP00-493 likewise set runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-494 (contract:16201), yet CP00-494 itself remains descriptor-only (manifest runtime_ready=false, no_*_runtime attestations true). The field is a forward-looking candidate marker, always gated by human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true and the open_scope text ('until downstream packs explicitly open runtime behavior'), not an assertion that the named pack opens runtime. The naming could be misread as a commitment that CP00-495 opens runtime.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout; the human-approval gate and descriptor-only boundary are intact. Optionally clarify the field name (e.g. next_runtime_open_candidate_pack_id) or document the convention so the forward-reference is not misread as a runtime-open commitment.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as by-design forward-reference, consistent across CP00-480..CP00-494; non-blocking. Human approver may note for naming clarity only.
